title: Creatine performance and lean-mass evidence refresh
review_notes: Reframes the current cognition-heavy summary around creatine's better-established exercise-performance evidence.
The postmenopausal-women findings are population-specific and are not generalized to all adults.
No evidence tier, dosage, safety, governance, or publishing-control fields are changed.

sources.title: Effects of combined versus single supplementation of creatine and beta-alanine on aerobic and anerobic performance: a systematic review and network meta-analysis
sources.journal: Journal of the International Society of Sports Nutrition

sources.title: Creatine monohydrate for lean mass, strength, and bone density in postmenopausal women: a systematic review and meta-analysis
sources.journal: Journal of the International Society of Sports Nutrition

changes.expected_old_value: May support memory and some attention/processing-speed outcomes, especially where brain energy demand is relevant.
changes.new_value: Creatine monohydrate has strong human evidence for selected high-intensity exercise outcomes and can modestly support lean mass and strength, particularly alongside resistance training. Cognitive findings are more context-dependent and do not establish a universal nootropic effect.
changes.rationale: Aligns the lead summary with the stronger exercise evidence while preserving a clear limit on broader cognitive claims.

changes.expected_old_value: May support memory and some attention/processing-speed outcomes, especially where brain energy demand is relevant. Caveat: Do not imply universal nootropic effects, ADHD treatment, dementia prevention, or guaranteed focus boost.
changes.new_value: Meta-analytic evidence supports creatine for several high-intensity performance outcomes. In postmenopausal women, trials suggest small gains in lean mass and strength, especially when at least 5 g/day was paired with resistance training, while bone-density effects remain unclear. These population-specific study regimens are not universal dosing advice.
changes.rationale: Adds quantified, population-specific context without converting a studied regimen into a general recommendation.
